verdict_reasoning: ABCB9 is a lysosomal ABC transporter (TAP-like protein) with a multi-pass transmembrane architecture. Its canonical localization is the lysosomal membrane. However, it has documented surface exposure during lysosomal exocytosis — a physiological process activated during immune cell degranulation, nutrient stress, and cell-death signaling. The protein body reaches the outer leaflet when lysosomal vesicles fuse with the plasma membrane, transiently exposing lysosomal-resident TM proteins on the cell surface. This qualifies as contextual surface accessibility via lysosomal-exocytosis-triggered PM recruitment.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Extent and stability of ABCB9 surface residence during exocytosis cycles — unclear whether the protein is rapidly re-internalized or remains stably accessible long enough for therapeutic engagement. Lysosomal exocytosis is context-dependent and cell-type-restricted.
